mechanism: For stimulus regions positioned at varying distances from foveal center, behavioral accuracy and response latency are measured via repeated categorization tasks with psychophysical controls. Performance degradation with increasing distance (eccentricity) is quantified as within-subject accuracy differences, analyzed through factorial designs crossing stimulus location and category dimensions.

An interactive choice-based narrative system exposes participants to instructional scenarios teaching pattern-recognition rules via positive reinforcement feedback. Pre-post survey design with embedded within-subject measurement. Participants rate reliability of stimuli on ordinal scales before and after algorithmic exposure to synthetic examples of deceptive messaging patterns.

Principal components analysis on trait ratings of face images extracts orthogonal latent dimensions. Linear parametric models in 50-dimensional face-shape space identify optimal directional changes in 3D vertex positions for manipulating estimated trait values. Validation through parametric face stimuli generation and perceptual judgment tasks.

Repeated familiarization and forced-choice identification tasks expose participants to triplet sequences conforming to abstract repetition patterns (e.g., AAB). Accuracy measured as proportion correct in binary choice between learned vs. foil patterns. Performance compared across within-subject variation in syntactic category membership and grammatical plausibility of target patterns.

Field observations and controlled experiments measure behavioral outcomes (traffic violation rates, good-taking, cheating magnitude) via binary or ordinal response. Mediation analysis using linear regression traces indirect effects of socioeconomic rank through measured attitudes. Multiple modalities (naturalistic observation, laboratory task, self-report scenario) applied to same putative construct.

Binary Bayesian decoding: posterior probability P(intended-sentence | perceived-sentence) computed by weighting prior P(intended) against channel-corruption likelihood P(perceived-given-intended). String-edit-distance model quantifies corruption as insertions and deletions. Comprehension tested via binary-choice question pairs; literal-interpretation rate varies with edit distance and noise conditions across alternation types.
